quick_wins: System Design: Novice to Beginner (~19 hours)
strengths_summary: Strong in 10 areas: Python: Intermediate (meets requirement); FastAPI: Intermediate (exceeds requirement); PostgreSQL: Advanced (exceeds requirement); Git: Intermediate (exceeds requirement); TypeScript: Intermediate (exceeds requirement)

learning_paths.why_learn: CI/CD is a core skill for any backend engineer. Automating your build, test, and deployment pipeline will make you a more productive contributor from day one.
leverage_existing: Your Docker experience means you already understand containerized environments that CI/CD pipelines deploy to.
Your Git proficiency means you are already comfortable with the branching and merging workflows that trigger CI/CD pipelines.

milestones.title: Understand CI/CD Fundamentals
milestones.description: Learn what continuous integration and deployment means, why it matters, and the key concepts.

resources.title: GitHub Actions Quickstart
resources.description: Official GitHub Actions tutorial to set up your first workflow

resources.title: CI/CD Pipeline Tutorial - TechWorld with Nana
resources.description: Comprehensive video walkthrough of CI/CD concepts and implementation

milestones.practice_project: Set up a GitHub Actions workflow for one of your existing projects that runs tests on every push and deploys to Vercel on merge to main.
